facts: Lionel Messi became the first player in history to score in seven consecutive tournament matches, extending his all-time record to 19 goals. — *ESPN*
Harry Kane became **England**'s all-time tournament top scorer with 11 goals, surpassing Gary Lineker's record of 10. — *BBC*

**Austria** reached the knockout stage for the first time since 1982, surviving a 96th-minute Kalajdzic equaliser against **Algeria** in one of the most dramatic finishes in group-stage history. — *BBC*
**Panama** became the only team out of all 48 sides to leave the tournament without scoring a single goal. — *BBC*
Today's match is the first-ever Round of 32 game in tournament history — the opening match of the expanded knockout format. — *ESPN*
Both **South Africa** and **Canada** are playing in a knockout match for the first time ever. — *BBC*
The two nations have met only once before — a friendly in 2007 — making this their first competitive encounter. — *ESPN*
**South Africa** never advanced past the group stage before this tournament, including when they hosted in 2010. — *BBC*
**Netherlands** vs **Morocco** falls exactly 32 years to the day since the two sides met at USA '94 — also on American soil. Current Dutch coach Ronald Koeman started that match. — *BBC*
**Brazil** have won 11 of their 14 all-time meetings with **Japan**, but **Japan** stunned them 3-2 in a friendly in October 2025 — their first-ever win over the five-time champions. — *ESPN*
**Germany**'s 10 group-stage goals came from seven different scorers — the most balanced attack at this tournament. Deniz Undav scored three without starting a single match. — *The Athletic*
**Morocco**'s 2022 semi-final run was the best finish by an African nation in tournament history; the **Netherlands** are three-time finalists who have never won it. — *BBC*

**Mexico** have not conceded a first-half goal in 15 consecutive tournament matches, a streak dating back to 2014. The last team to score against them before halftime was **Argentina** in the 2010 R16 (Tevez 26′, Higuain 33′). — *The Athletic*

Haaland has scored 60 international goals in just 53 caps — reaching that mark in 69 fewer appearances than Messi and 77 fewer than Ronaldo. All five of his tournament goals have been one-touch finishes. — *BBC*
**France** are the first team to score 3+ goals in five straight tournament matches. The Dembele-Mbappe duo have combined for 6 goals — the most by any pair on record since 1966. — *BBC*
Gilberto Mora (17 years, 259 days) became the youngest player to start a knockout match since Pele (17 years, 239 days) in 1958. — *The Athletic*

The **USA**'s win over **Bosnia** was only their second-ever knockout victory — the first since 2002 — and the first ever in primetime on home soil. It was also the first time the US has won three games at a single tournament. *(The Athletic, Paul Tenorio)*
Balogun's three tournament goals tie him for second on the all-time US list with Landon Donovan (2010). Only Bert Patenaude's four in 1930 are more. *(The Athletic, Henry Bushnell)*

Red cards at the tournament cannot be appealed — FIFA Article 66.4 prohibits it. Balogun is automatically suspended for the **Belgium** R16. Former PL referee Graham Scott called it "unlucky but inevitable" — VAR slow-motion distorts incidental contact. *(The Athletic, Adam Crafton)*

Kane became the first **England** player to score twice in a tournament knockout match since Lineker vs Cameroon in 1990. He is now joint 6th all-time with 13 tournament goals — level with Pelé. *(BBC Sport, Phil McNulty)*

**Belgium** rallied from 2-0 down against **Senegal** — scoring twice in 159 seconds through Lukaku (87') and Tielemans (89'). Tielemans' 125th-minute penalty is the latest goal in tournament history. *(The Athletic, Tim Spiers)*
Tillman waved off Pulisic — who wanted to take the free kick — and curled it in himself from 25 yards in the 82nd minute to seal the **USA** win with 10 men. *(The Athletic, Tom Bogert)*

**Austria** are appearing in the Round of 32 at the finals for the first time since 1998 — a 28-year absence from the knockout rounds that ended when Sasa Kalajdzic headed in a 96th-minute equaliser against **Algeria**. — *Sports Mole*
**Portugal** and **Croatia** have never previously met in a knockout match at the finals, despite both nations being regular tournament fixtures since the 1990s. — *RotoWire*
**Switzerland** are aiming to reach the Round of 16 for the fourth consecutive time, a feat that would equal their longest-ever streak at this stage of the competition. — *Sports Mole*

**Algeria** manager Vladimir Petković spent seven years as head coach of **Switzerland** before taking the Algeria job, making Thursday's Round of 32 tie a rare coaching reunion at the finals. — *OneFootball*

**Spain** have kept clean sheets in eight of their last nine competitive fixtures and are yet to concede a single goal at this tournament — but arrive at the knockouts with all three first-choice wide forwards carrying fitness doubts. — *Fox Sports*
